title: swida macrophylla var. longipedunculata
intro: Swida macrophylla var. longipedunculata, also known as a variety of the large-leaved guelder rose, is a plant species that belongs to the honeysuckle family (Caprifoliaceae). It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which make it useful in treating various health issues. The plant has been traditionally used to treat fever, colds, and respiratory infections due to its ability to reduce inflammation and fight off viral and bacterial infections. Herbal preparations of Swida macrophylla var. longipedunculata include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and ointments for topical application.
